rationale: In the methods section, the cited paper is explicitly used to justify scaling household calories by adult-equivalent nutritional needs when constructing a primary dependent variable. That role is functionally important to the empirical setup, not just broad background. At the same time, the citing paper does not extend or contest the cited paper's minimum-wage arguments; it mainly reuses an operationalization choice in a different policy context.
relation_description: The citing paper applies a measurement approach from Palazzolo and Pattabhiramaiah (2021) to normalize grocery calorie purchases by household adult-equivalent needs. This is a transfer of operationalization to study HHFKA effects rather than a direct extension of the minimum-wage research question.
